title: Audience Contamination

content.statement: Algorithms learn from your inputs, even bad ones.
content.question: Is your business's ad targeting teaching Meta or TikTok to find real customers, or tourists who look similar?
content.equation: AC = Wrong Audience % × Reinforcement Rate × CPM
sop: Pull your last 90 days of leads or customers and tag each as 'ideal buyer' or 'browsing/low-fit.'
Check what percentage of your ad spend or leads came from broad or lookalike audiences versus narrow targeting.
Rate your Wrong Audience percentage and Reinforcement Rate (how aggressively you're optimizing toward that group) (see calculation, page 6).
Exclude your lowest-quality lead segment from future campaigns instead of just deprioritizing it.
Feed the algorithm a 'best customer' seed list built from past buyers only, instead of broad conversion events.
content.explainer: Bad leads don't just waste money today; they train the algorithm to bring more bad leads tomorrow.
questions: Is your business's ad targeting teaching Meta or TikTok to find real customers, or tourists who look similar?
How many of your last 100 leads would you actually want to talk to, versus just call to hit a number?
If you paused all broad or lookalike targeting for a month, would your lead quality go up or your volume just disappear?

definitions.def: Cost per thousand ad impressions, what you pay to show your ad 1,000 times.
An ad-targeting group the platform builds by finding new people who resemble your existing customers.

content.equation_example: Wrong Audience percentage 30%, Reinforcement Rate 1.5 (how aggressively the algorithm is chasing that audience), CPM $12. 0.30 times 1.5 times 12 equals $5.40. Higher means more of your ad budget is being spent training the algorithm toward the wrong audience; lower means your targeting is staying clean.
content.closing_statement: What you click on is what you'll get more of.

caption:
Principle Nº 94: Audience Contamination

Algorithms learn from your inputs, even bad ones.

Ask yourself: Is your business's ad targeting teaching Meta or TikTok to find real customers, or tourists who look similar?
